“House of Millions of Years” }} [[File:Karte grabstätten theben west.png|thumb|Temple landscape on the west bank of the Nile at Thebes]] The '''million‑year house''' (also '''house of millions of years, Millionenjahrhaus''') is a term that first appears at the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} and originally denoted a temple for the royal cult. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Their imagery – both in cult statues and wall reliefs – presents a mystical union of the king with a major deity such as {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|WD=Q58373|lt=Amun}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris}}, or {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah}}, believed to guarantee the monarch’s eternal existence.citeturn0 Most million‑year houses once stood parallel to the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile}}, opposite the {{ill|Valley of the Kings|en|Valley of the Kings|WD=Q133423|lt=Valley of the Kings}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben-West|ja|テーベ西岸|zh|底比斯西岸|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; others were erected at {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis, Egypt}} ({{ill|Amenhotep III|en|Amenhotep III|WD=Q42606|lt=Amenhotep III}}, {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II}}), {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt}} ({{ill|Seti I|en|Seti I|WD=Q157918|lt=Seti I}}, Ramesses II), {{ill|Soleb|en|Soleb|WD=Q1457914|lt=Soleb}} (Amenhotep III) and {{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Abu Simbel temples}} (Ramesses II). Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu}}, temple of {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramesses III}}]] === Thebes‑West === *Rock‑cut temples at {{ill|Deir el-Bahari|en|Deir Die Häuser der Millionen von Jahren: Eine Untersuchung zu Königskult und Tempeltypologie in Ägypten'', Wiesbaden 2002, ISBN 3‑447‑04521‑3. * Stefanie Schröder, ''Millionenjahrhaus. Zur Konzeption des Raumes der Ewigkeit …'', Wiesbaden 2010, ISBN 978‑3‑447‑06187‑2. == Notes == {{Coordinate|NS=25/44/17/N|EW=32/37/53/E|type=landmark|region=EG}} [[de:Haus der Millionen]] [[de:Millionenjahrhaus]] {{translated page|de|Haus der Millionen|version=243359763|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ar:بيت المليون سنة]] [[arz:بيت المليون سنه]] [[ca:Temple del milió d'anys]] [[es:Casa del millón de años]] [[fr:Temple des millions d'années]] [[it:Tempio di Milioni di Anni]] {{wikidata link|Q13466456}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Million-year houses]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

[[ar:بيت المليون سنة]][[arz:بيت المليون سنه]][[ca:Temple del milió d'anys]][[de:Haus der Millionen]][[es:Casa del millón de años]][[fr:Temple des millions d'années]][[it:Tempio di Milioni di Anni]] [[ar:بيت المليون سنة]][[arz:بيت المليون سنه]][[ca:Temple del milió d'anys]][[de:Haus der Millionen]][[es:Casa del millón de años]][[fr:Temple des millions d'années]][[it:Tempio di Milioni di Anni]] [[ar:بيت المليون سنة]][[arz:بيت المليون سنه]][[ca:Temple del milió d'anys]][[de:Haus der Millionen]][[es:Casa del millón de años]][[fr:Temple des millions d'années]][[it:Tempio di Milioni di Anni]] {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |TITEL = Million‑year house |NAME = O6-X1:O1-N35:X1-C11-Z3-G17-M4-M4-M4
Hut‑net‑hehu‑em‑renput
''Ḥw.t‑n.t‑ḥḥw‑m‑rnp.wt''
“House of Millions of Years” }} [[File:Karte grabstätten theben west.png|thumb|Temple landscape on the west bank of the Nile at Thebes]] The '''million‑year house''' (also '''house of millions of years, Millionenjahrhaus''') is a term that first appears at the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} and originally denoted a temple for the royal cult. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Their imagery – both in cult statues and wall reliefs – presents a mystical union of the king with a major deity such as {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|WD=Q58373|lt=Amun}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris}}, or {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah}}, believed to guarantee the monarch’s eternal existence.citeturn0 Most million‑year houses once stood parallel to the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile}}, opposite the {{ill|Valley of the Kings|en|Valley of the Kings|WD=Q133423|lt=Valley of the Kings}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben-West|ja|テーベ西岸|zh|底比斯西岸|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; others were erected at {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis, Egypt}} ({{ill|Amenhotep III|en|Amenhotep III|WD=Q42606|lt=Amenhotep III}}, {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II}}), {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt}} ({{ill|Seti I|en|Seti I|WD=Q157918|lt=Seti I}}, Ramesses II), {{ill|Soleb|en|Soleb|WD=Q1457914|lt=Soleb}} (Amenhotep III) and {{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Abu Simbel temples}} (Ramesses II). Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, Egypt, Temple of Hatshepsut.jpg|mini|Temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut}}]] [[Datei:Luxor, Medinet Habu, Egypt, Oct 2004 A.jpg|mini|Entrance to {{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu}}, temple of {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramesses III}}]] === Thebes‑West === *Rock‑cut temples at {{ill|Deir el-Bahari|en|Deir el-Bahari|WD=Q373909|lt=Deir el-Bahari}}* * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Mentuhotep II|de|Totentempel des Mentuhotep II.|ja|メンチュホテプ2世葬祭殿|zh|蒙图霍特普二世葬祭殿|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|en|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|WD=Q660692|lt=Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut}} (*Djeser‑djeseru* – “Holiest of Holies”) * [[:en:Temple of Thutmose III|Mortuary Temple of Thutmose III (Deir el-Bahari)]] *Million‑year houses at {{ill|Qurna|de|el-Qurna|ja|エル・クルナ|zh|古尔纳|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Seti I|en|Mortuary Temple of Seti I|WD=Q375642|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Qurna)}} * {{ill|Temple of the Ramesside kings (Asasif)|de|Tempel der Ramessiden (Asasif)|ja|ラメシデ朝神殿 (アサシフ)|zh|拉美西德王神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III}} (el‑Qurna) * Temple of {{ill|Siptah|en|Siptah|WD=Q158059|lt=Ramesses Siptah}} * Temple of {{ill|Amenhotep II|en|Amenhotep II|WD=Q158052|lt=Amenhotep II}} * The {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum}} (temple of Ramesses II) * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose IV|en|Thutmose IV|WD=Q157985|lt=Thutmose IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Tausret|en|Tausret|WD=Q158012|lt=Twosret}} * Temple of {{ill|Merneptah|en|Merneptah|WD=Q158043|lt=Merneptah}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|en|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|WD=Q1058753|lt=Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III}} (behind the Colossi of Memnon) * Temple of {{ill|Ramesses IV|en|Ramesses IV|WD=Q1532|lt=Ramesses IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose II|en|Thutmose II|WD=Q158025|lt=Thutmose II}} * Temple of {{ill|Horemheb|en|Horemheb|WD=Q157995|lt=Horemheb}}, {{ill|Ay|en|Ay|WD=Q234187|lt=Ay}}, and {{ill|Tutankhamun|en|Tutankhamun|WD=Q12154|lt=Tutankhamun}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|en|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|WD=Q656040|lt=Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III}} ({{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu}}) === Abydos === * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses II (Abydos)|de|Tempel des Ramses II. von Abydos|ja|ラムセス2世葬祭殿 (アビドス)|zh|拉美西斯二世葬祭殿 (阿比多斯)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}: *Hut‑abdu‑Men‑maat‑Ra* (Ḥwt‑3bḏw‑mn‑mȝʿ.t‑Rʿ) – “House of Men‑maat‑Ra in Abydos” == See also == * {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|en|Thebes, Egypt|WD=Q101583|lt=Thebes, Egypt}} * {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt}} == Literature == * Martina Ullmann, ''König für die Ewigkeit – Die Häuser der Millionen von Jahren: Eine Untersuchung zu Königskult und Tempeltypologie in Ägypten'', Wiesbaden 2002, ISBN 3‑447‑04521‑3. * Stefanie Schröder, ''Millionenjahrhaus. Zur Konzeption des Raumes der Ewigkeit …'', Wiesbaden 2010, ISBN 978‑3‑447‑06187‑2. == Notes == {{Coordinate|NS=25/44/17/N|EW=32/37/53/E|type=landmark|region=EG}} [[de:Haus der Millionen]] [[de:Millionenjahrhaus]] {{translated page|de|Haus der Millionen|version=243359763|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ar:بيت المليون سنة]] [[arz:بيت المليون سنه]] [[ca:Temple del milió d'anys]] [[es:Casa del millón de años]] [[fr:Temple des millions d'années]] [[it:Tempio di Milioni di Anni]] {{wikidata link|Q13466456}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Million-year houses]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

“House of Millions of Years” }} [[File:Karte grabstätten theben west.png|thumb|Temple landscape on the west bank of the Nile at Thebes]] The '''million‑year house''' (also '''house of millions of years, Millionenjahrhaus''') is a term that first appears at the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} and originally denoted a temple for the royal cult. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Their imagery – both in cult statues and wall reliefs – presents a mystical union of the king with a major deity such as {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|WD=Q58373|lt=Amun}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris}}, or {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah}}, believed to guarantee the monarch’s eternal existence.citeturn0 Most million‑year houses once stood parallel to the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile}}, opposite the {{ill|Valley of the Kings|en|Valley of the Kings|WD=Q133423|lt=Valley of the Kings}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben-West|ja|テーベ西岸|zh|底比斯西岸|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; others were erected at {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis, Egypt}} ({{ill|Amenhotep III|en|Amenhotep III|WD=Q42606|lt=Amenhotep III}}, {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II}}), {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt}} ({{ill|Seti I|en|Seti I|WD=Q157918|lt=Seti I}}, Ramesses II), {{ill|Soleb|en|Soleb|WD=Q1457914|lt=Soleb}} (Amenhotep III) and {{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Abu Simbel temples}} (Ramesses II). Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, “House of Millions of Years” }} [[File:Karte grabstätten theben west.png|thumb|Temple landscape on the west bank of the Nile at Thebes]] The '''million‑year house''' (also '''house of millions of years, Millionenjahrhaus''') is a term that first appears at the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} and originally denoted a temple for the royal cult. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Their imagery – both in cult statues and wall reliefs – presents a mystical union of the king with a major deity such as {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|WD=Q58373|lt=Amun}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris}}, or {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah}}, believed to guarantee the monarch’s eternal existence.citeturn0 Most million‑year houses once stood parallel to the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile}}, opposite the {{ill|Valley of the Kings|en|Valley of the Kings|WD=Q133423|lt=Valley of the Kings}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben-West|ja|テーベ西岸|zh|底比斯西岸|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; others were erected at {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis, Egypt}} ({{ill|Amenhotep III|en|Amenhotep III|WD=Q42606|lt=Amenhotep III}}, {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II}}), {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt}} ({{ill|Seti I|en|Seti I|WD=Q157918|lt=Seti I}}, Ramesses II), {{ill|Soleb|en|Soleb|WD=Q1457914|lt=Soleb}} (Amenhotep III) and {{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Abu Simbel temples}} (Ramesses II). Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, Egypt, Temple of Hatshepsut.jpg|mini|Temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut}}]] [[Datei:Luxor, Medinet Habu, Egypt, Oct 2004 A.jpg|mini|Entrance to {{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu}}, temple of {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramesses III}}]] === Thebes‑West === *Rock‑cut temples at {{ill|Deir el-Bahari|en|Deir el-Bahari|WD=Q373909|lt=Deir el-Bahari}}* * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Mentuhotep II|de|Totentempel des Mentuhotep II.|ja|メンチュホテプ2世葬祭殿|zh|蒙图霍特普二世葬祭殿|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|en|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|WD=Q660692|lt=Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut}} (*Djeser‑djeseru* – “Holiest of Holies”) * [[:en:Temple of Thutmose III|Mortuary Temple of Thutmose III (Deir el-Bahari)]] *Million‑year houses at {{ill|Qurna|de|el-Qurna|ja|エル・クルナ|zh|古尔纳|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Seti I|en|Mortuary Temple of Seti I|WD=Q375642|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Qurna)}} * {{ill|Temple of the Ramesside kings (Asasif)|de|Tempel der Ramessiden (Asasif)|ja|ラメシデ朝神殿 (アサシフ)|zh|拉美西德王神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III}} (el‑Qurna) * Temple of {{ill|Siptah|en|Siptah|WD=Q158059|lt=Ramesses Siptah}} * Temple of {{ill|Amenhotep II|en|Amenhotep II|WD=Q158052|lt=Amenhotep II}} * The {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum}} (temple of Ramesses II) * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose IV|en|Thutmose IV|WD=Q157985|lt=Thutmose IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Tausret|en|Tausret|WD=Q158012|lt=Twosret}} * Temple of {{ill|Merneptah|en|Merneptah|WD=Q158043|lt=Merneptah}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|en|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|WD=Q1058753|lt=Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III}} (behind the Colossi of Memnon) * Temple of {{ill|Ramesses IV|en|Ramesses IV|WD=Q1532|lt=Ramesses IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose II|en|Thutmose II|WD=Q158025|lt=Thutmose II}} * Temple of {{ill|Horemheb|en|Horemheb|WD=Q157995|lt=Horemheb}}, {{ill|Ay|en|Ay|WD=Q234187|lt=Ay}}, and {{ill|Tutankhamun|en|Tutankhamun|WD=Q12154|lt=Tutankhamun}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|en|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|WD=Q656040|lt=Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III}} ({{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu}}) === Abydos === * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses II (Abydos)|de|Tempel des Ramses II. von Abydos|ja|ラムセス2世葬祭殿 (アビドス)|zh|拉美西斯二世葬祭殿 (阿比多斯)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}: *Hut‑abdu‑Men‑maat‑Ra* (Ḥwt‑3bḏw‑mn‑mȝʿ.t‑Rʿ) – “House of Men‑maat‑Ra in Abydos” == See also == * {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|en|Thebes, Egypt|WD=Q101583|lt=Thebes, Egypt}} * {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt}} == Literature == * Martina Ullmann, ''König für die Ewigkeit – Die Häuser der Millionen von Jahren: Eine Untersuchung zu Königskult und Tempeltypologie in Ägypten'', Wiesbaden 2002, ISBN 3‑447‑04521‑3. * Stefanie Schröder, ''Millionenjahrhaus. Zur Konzeption des Raumes der Ewigkeit …'', Wiesbaden 2010, ISBN 978‑3‑447‑06187‑2. == Notes == {{Coordinate|NS=25/44/17/N|EW=32/37/53/E|type=landmark|region=EG}} [[de:Haus der Millionen]] [[de:Millionenjahrhaus]] {{translated page|de|Haus der Millionen|version=243359763|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ar:بيت المليون سنة]] [[arz:بيت المليون سنه]] [[ca:Temple del milió d'anys]] [[es:Casa del millón de años]] [[fr:Temple des millions d'années]] [[it:Tempio di Milioni di Anni]] {{wikidata link|Q13466456}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Million-year houses]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:pages]]

In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple|1=Mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Their imagery – both in cult statues and wall reliefs – presents a mystical union of the king with a major deity such as {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|WD=Q58373|lt=Amun|1=Amun}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}, or {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah|1=Ptah}}, believed to guarantee the monarch’s eternal existence.citeturn0 Most million‑year houses once stood parallel to the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}}, opposite the {{ill|Valley of the Kings|en|Valley of the Kings|WD=Q133423|lt=Valley of the Kings|1=Valley of the Kings}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben-West|ja|テーベ西岸|zh|底比斯西岸|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; others were erected at {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis, Egypt|1=Memphis, Egypt}} ({{ill|Amenhotep III|en|Amenhotep III|WD=Q42606|lt=Amenhotep III|1=Amenhotep III}}, {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II|1=Ramesses II}}), {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, Egypt}} ({{ill|Seti I|en|Seti I|WD=Q157918|lt=Seti I|1=Seti I}}, Ramesses II), {{ill|Soleb|en|Soleb|WD=Q1457914|lt=Soleb|1=Soleb}} (Amenhotep III) and {{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Abu Simbel temples|1=Abu Simbel}} (Ramesses II). Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs|1=Pharaoh}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid|1=Pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn|1=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon|1=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, Egypt, Temple of Hatshepsut.jpg|mini|Temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}]] [[Datei:Luxor, Medinet Habu, Egypt, Oct 2004 A.jpg|mini|Entrance to {{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu|1=Medinet Habu}}, temple of {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramesses III|1=Ramesses III}}]] === In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple|1=Mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. “House of Millions of Years” }} [[File:Karte grabstätten theben west.png|thumb|Temple landscape on the west bank of the Nile at Thebes]] The '''million‑year house''' (also '''house of millions of years, Millionenjahrhaus''') is a term that first appears at the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} and originally denoted a temple for the royal cult. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple|1=Mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Their imagery – both in cult statues and wall reliefs – presents a mystical union of the king with a major deity such as {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|WD=Q58373|lt=Amun|1=Amun}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}, or {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah|1=Ptah}}, believed to guarantee the monarch’s eternal existence.citeturn0 Most million‑year houses once stood parallel to the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}}, opposite the {{ill|Valley of the Kings|en|Valley of the Kings|WD=Q133423|lt=Valley of the Kings|1=Valley of the Kings}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben-West|ja|テーベ西岸|zh|底比斯西岸|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; others were erected at {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis, Egypt|1=Memphis, Egypt}} ({{ill|Amenhotep III|en|Amenhotep III|WD=Q42606|lt=Amenhotep III|1=Amenhotep III}}, {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II|1=Ramesses II}}), {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, Egypt}} ({{ill|Seti I|en|Seti I|WD=Q157918|lt=Seti I|1=Seti I}}, Ramesses II), {{ill|Soleb|en|Soleb|WD=Q1457914|lt=Soleb|1=Soleb}} (Amenhotep III) and {{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Abu Simbel temples|1=Abu Simbel}} (Ramesses II). Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs|1=Pharaoh}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid|1=Pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn|1=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon|1=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, Egypt, Temple of Hatshepsut.jpg|mini|Temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}]] [[Datei:Luxor, Medinet Habu, Egypt, Oct 2004 A.jpg|mini|Entrance to {{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu|1=Medinet Habu}}, temple of {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramesses III|1=Ramesses III}}]] === “House of Millions of Years” }} [[File:Karte grabstätten theben west.png|thumb|Temple landscape on the west bank of the Nile at Thebes]] The '''million‑year house''' (also '''house of millions of years, Millionenjahrhaus''') is a term that first appears at the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} and originally denoted a temple for the royal cult. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple|1=Mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Their imagery – both in cult statues and wall reliefs – presents a mystical union of the king with a major deity such as {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|WD=Q58373|lt=Amun|1=Amun}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}, or {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah|1=Ptah}}, believed to guarantee the monarch’s eternal existence.citeturn0 Most million‑year houses once stood parallel to the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}}, opposite the {{ill|Valley of the Kings|en|Valley of the Kings|WD=Q133423|lt=Valley of the Kings|1=Valley of the Kings}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben-West|ja|テーベ西岸|zh|底比斯西岸|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; others were erected at {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis, Egypt|1=Memphis, Egypt}} ({{ill|Amenhotep III|en|Amenhotep III|WD=Q42606|lt=Amenhotep III|1=Amenhotep III}}, {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II|1=Ramesses II}}), {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, Egypt}} ({{ill|Seti I|en|Seti I|WD=Q157918|lt=Seti I|1=Seti I}}, Ramesses II), {{ill|Soleb|en|Soleb|WD=Q1457914|lt=Soleb|1=Soleb}} (Amenhotep III) and {{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Abu Simbel temples|1=Abu Simbel}} (Ramesses II). Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs|1=Pharaoh}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid|1=Pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn|1=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon|1=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, Egypt, Temple of Hatshepsut.jpg|mini|Temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}]] [[Datei:Luxor, Medinet Habu, Egypt, Oct 2004 A.jpg|mini|Entrance to {{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu|1=Medinet Habu}}, temple of {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramesses III|1=Ramesses III}}]] === Thebes‑West === *Rock‑cut temples at {{ill|Deir el-Bahari|en|Deir el-Bahari|WD=Q373909|lt=Deir el-Bahari|1=Deir el-Bahari}}* * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Mentuhotep II|de|Totentempel des Mentuhotep II.|ja|メンチュホテプ2世葬祭殿|zh|蒙图霍特普二世葬祭殿|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|en|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|WD=Q660692|lt=Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut|1=Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut}} (*Djeser‑djeseru* – “Holiest of Holies”) * [[:en:Temple of Thutmose III|Mortuary Temple of Thutmose III (Deir el-Bahari)]] *Million‑year houses at {{ill|Qurna|de|el-Qurna|ja|エル・クルナ|zh|古尔纳|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Seti I|en|Mortuary Temple of Seti I|WD=Q375642|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Qurna)|1=Mortuary Temple of Seti I}} * {{ill|Temple of the Ramesside kings (Asasif)|de|Tempel der Ramessiden (Asasif)|ja|ラメシデ朝神殿 (アサシフ)|zh|拉美西德王神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III|1=Thutmose III}} (el‑Qurna) * Temple of {{ill|Siptah|en|Siptah|WD=Q158059|lt=Ramesses Siptah|1=Siptah}} * Temple of {{ill|Amenhotep II|en|Amenhotep II|WD=Q158052|lt=Amenhotep II|1=Amenhotep II}} * The {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}} (temple of Ramesses II) * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose IV|en|Thutmose IV|WD=Q157985|lt=Thutmose IV|1=Thutmose IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Tausret|en|Tausret|WD=Q158012|lt=Twosret|1=Tausret}} * Temple of {{ill|Merneptah|en|Merneptah|WD=Q158043|lt=Merneptah|1=Merneptah}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|en|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|WD=Q1058753|lt=Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|1=Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III}} (behind the Colossi of Memnon) * Temple of {{ill|Ramesses IV|en|Ramesses IV|WD=Q1532|lt=Ramesses IV|1=Ramesses IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose II|en|Thutmose II|WD=Q158025|lt=Thutmose II|1=Thutmose II}} * Temple of {{ill|Horemheb|en|Horemheb|WD=Q157995|lt=Horemheb|1=Horemheb}}, {{ill|Ay|en|Ay|WD=Q234187|lt=Ay|1=Ay}}, and {{ill|Tutankhamun|en|Tutankhamun|WD=Q12154|lt=Tutankhamun|1=Tutankhamun}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|en|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|WD=Q656040|lt=Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|1=Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III}} ({{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu|1=Medinet Habu}}) === Abydos === * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses II (Abydos)|de|Tempel des Ramses II. von Abydos|ja|ラムセス2世葬祭殿 (アビドス)|zh|拉美西斯二世葬祭殿 (阿比多斯)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|1=Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}: *Hut‑abdu‑Men‑maat‑Ra* (Ḥwt‑3bḏw‑mn‑mȝʿ.t‑Rʿ) – “House of Men‑maat‑Ra in Abydos” == See also == * {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|en|Thebes, Egypt|WD=Q101583|lt=Thebes, Egypt|1=Thebes, Egypt}} * {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, Egypt}} == Literature == * Martina Ullmann, ''König für die Ewigkeit – Die Häuser der Millionen von Jahren: Eine Untersuchung zu Königskult und Tempeltypologie in Ägypten'', Wiesbaden 2002, ISBN Zur Konzeption des Raumes der Ewigkeit …'', Wiesbaden 2010, ISBN 978‑3‑447‑06187‑2. == Notes == {{Coordinate|NS=25/44/17/N|EW=32/37/53/E|type=landmark|region=EG}} [[de:Haus der Millionen]] [[de:Millionenjahrhaus]] {{translated page|de|Haus der Millionen|version=243359763|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ar:بيت المليون سنة]] [[arz:بيت المليون سنه]] [[ca:Temple del milió d'anys]] [[es:Casa del millón de años]] [[fr:Temple des millions d'années]] [[it:Tempio di Milioni di Anni]] {{wikidata link|Q13466456}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Million-year houses]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

“House of Millions of Years” }} [[File:Karte grabstätten theben west.png|thumb|Temple landscape on the west bank of the Nile at Thebes]] The '''million‑year house''' (also '''house of millions of years, Millionenjahrhaus''') is a term that first appears at the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} and originally denoted a temple for the royal cult. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple|1=Mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Their imagery – both in cult statues and wall reliefs – presents a mystical union of the king with a major deity such as {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|WD=Q58373|lt=Amun|1=Amun}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}, or {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah|1=Ptah}}, believed to guarantee the monarch’s eternal existence.citeturn0 Most million‑year houses once stood parallel to the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}}, opposite the {{ill|Valley of the Kings|en|Valley of the Kings|WD=Q133423|lt=Valley of the Kings|1=Valley of the Kings}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben-West|ja|テーベ西岸|zh|底比斯西岸|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; others were erected at {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis, Egypt|1=Memphis, Egypt}} ({{ill|Amenhotep III|en|Amenhotep III|WD=Q42606|lt=Amenhotep III|1=Amenhotep III}}, {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II|1=Ramesses II}}), {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, Egypt}} ({{ill|Seti I|en|Seti I|WD=Q157918|lt=Seti I|1=Seti I}}, Ramesses II), {{ill|Soleb|en|Soleb|WD=Q1457914|lt=Soleb|1=Soleb}} (Amenhotep III) and {{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Abu Simbel temples|1=Abu Simbel}} (Ramesses II). Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs|1=Pharaoh}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid|1=Pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn|1=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon|1=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, Egypt, Temple of Hatshepsut.jpg|mini|Temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}]] [[Datei:Luxor, Medinet Habu, Egypt, Oct 2004 A.jpg|mini|Entrance to {{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu|1=Medinet Habu}}, temple of {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramesses III|1=Ramesses III}}]] === Thebes‑West === *Rock‑cut temples at {{ill|Deir el-Bahari|en|Deir el-Bahari|WD=Q373909|lt=Deir el-Bahari|1=Deir el-Bahari}}* * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Mentuhotep II|de|Totentempel des Mentuhotep II.|ja|メンチュホテプ2世葬祭殿|zh|蒙图霍特普二世葬祭殿|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|en|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|WD=Q660692|lt=Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut|1=Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut}} (*Djeser‑djeseru* – “Holiest of Holies”) * [[:en:Temple of Thutmose III|Mortuary Temple of Thutmose III (Deir el-Bahari)]] *Million‑year houses at Temple of Seti I}} * {{ill|Temple of the Ramesside kings (Asasif)|de|Tempel der Ramessiden (Asasif)|ja|ラメシデ朝神殿 (アサシフ)|zh|拉美西德王神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III|1=Thutmose III}} (el‑Qurna) * Temple of {{ill|Siptah|en|Siptah|WD=Q158059|lt=Ramesses Siptah|1=Siptah}} * Temple of {{ill|Amenhotep II|en|Amenhotep II|WD=Q158052|lt=Amenhotep II|1=Amenhotep II}} * The {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}} (temple of Ramesses II) * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose IV|en|Thutmose IV|WD=Q157985|lt=Thutmose IV|1=Thutmose IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Tausret|en|Tausret|WD=Q158012|lt=Twosret|1=Tausret}} * Temple of {{ill|Merneptah|en|Merneptah|WD=Q158043|lt=Merneptah|1=Merneptah}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|en|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|WD=Q1058753|lt=Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|1=Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III}} (behind the Colossi of Memnon) * Temple of {{ill|Ramesses IV|en|Ramesses IV|WD=Q1532|lt=Ramesses IV|1=Ramesses IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose II|en|Thutmose II|WD=Q158025|lt=Thutmose II|1=Thutmose II}} * Temple of {{ill|Horemheb|en|Horemheb|WD=Q157995|lt=Horemheb|1=Horemheb}}, {{ill|Ay|en|Ay|WD=Q234187|lt=Ay|1=Ay}}, and {{ill|Tutankhamun|en|Tutankhamun|WD=Q12154|lt=Tutankhamun|1=Tutankhamun}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|en|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|WD=Q656040|lt=Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|1=Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III}} ({{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu|1=Medinet Habu}}) === Abydos === * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses II (Abydos)|de|Tempel des Ramses II. von Abydos|ja|ラムセス2世葬祭殿 (アビドス)|zh|拉美西斯二世葬祭殿 (阿比多斯)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|1=Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}: *Hut‑abdu‑Men‑maat‑Ra* (Ḥwt‑3bḏw‑mn‑mȝʿ.t‑Rʿ) – “House of Men‑maat‑Ra in Abydos” == See also == * {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|en|Thebes, Egypt|WD=Q101583|lt=Thebes, Egypt|1=Thebes, Egypt}} * {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, Egypt}} == Literature == * Martina Ullmann, ''König für die Ewigkeit – Die Häuser der Millionen von Jahren: Eine Untersuchung zu Königskult und Tempeltypologie in Ägypten'', Wiesbaden 2002, ISBN In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple|1=Mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn|1=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon|1=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, Egypt, Temple of Hatshepsut.jpg|mini|Temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}]] [[Datei:Luxor, Medinet Habu, Egypt, Oct 2004 “House of Millions of Years” }} [[File:Karte grabstätten theben west.png|thumb|Temple landscape on the west bank of the Nile at Thebes]] The '''million‑year house''' (also '''house of millions of years, Millionenjahrhaus''') is a term that first appears at the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} and originally denoted a temple for the royal cult. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple|1=Mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Their imagery – both in cult statues and wall reliefs – presents a mystical union of the king with a major deity such as {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|WD=Q58373|lt=Amun|1=Amun}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}, or {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah|1=Ptah}}, believed to guarantee the monarch’s eternal existence.citeturn0 Most million‑year houses once stood parallel to the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}}, opposite the {{ill|Valley of the Kings|en|Valley of the Kings|WD=Q133423|lt=Valley of the Kings|1=Valley of the Kings}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben-West|ja|テーベ西岸|zh|底比斯西岸|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; others were erected at {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis, Egypt|1=Memphis, Egypt}} ({{ill|Amenhotep III|en|Amenhotep III|WD=Q42606|lt=Amenhotep III|1=Amenhotep III}}, {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II|1=Ramesses II}}), {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, Egypt}} ({{ill|Seti I|en|Seti I|WD=Q157918|lt=Seti I|1=Seti I}}, Ramesses II), {{ill|Soleb|en|Soleb|WD=Q1457914|lt=Soleb|1=Soleb}} (Amenhotep III) and {{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Abu Simbel temples|1=Abu Simbel}} (Ramesses II). Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs|1=Pharaoh}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid|1=Pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn|1=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon|1=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, Egypt, Temple of Hatshepsut.jpg|mini|Temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}]] [[Datei:Luxor, Medinet Habu, Egypt, Oct 2004 A.jpg|mini|Entrance to {{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu|1=Medinet Habu}}, temple of {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramesses III|1=Ramesses III}}]] === Thebes‑West === *Rock‑cut temples at {{ill|Deir el-Bahari|en|Deir el-Bahari|WD=Q373909|lt=Deir el-Bahari|1=Deir el-Bahari}}* * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Mentuhotep II|de|Totentempel des Mentuhotep II.|ja|メンチュホテプ2世葬祭殿|zh|蒙图霍特普二世葬祭殿|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|en|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|WD=Q660692|lt=Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut|1=Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut}} (*Djeser‑djeseru* – “Holiest of Holies”) * [[:en:Temple of Thutmose III|Mortuary Temple of Thutmose III (Deir el-Bahari)]] *Million‑year houses at Temple of Seti I}} * {{ill|Temple of the Ramesside kings (Asasif)|de|Tempel der Ramessiden (Asasif)|ja|ラメシデ朝神殿 (アサシフ)|zh|拉美西德王神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III|1=Thutmose III}} (el‑Qurna) * Temple of {{ill|Siptah|en|Siptah|WD=Q158059|lt=Ramesses Siptah|1=Siptah}} * Temple of {{ill|Amenhotep II|en|Amenhotep II|WD=Q158052|lt=Amenhotep II|1=Amenhotep II}} * The {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}} (temple of Ramesses II) * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose IV|en|Thutmose IV|WD=Q157985|lt=Thutmose IV|1=Thutmose IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Tausret|en|Tausret|WD=Q158012|lt=Twosret|1=Tausret}} * Temple of {{ill|Merneptah|en|Merneptah|WD=Q158043|lt=Merneptah|1=Merneptah}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|en|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|WD=Q1058753|lt=Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|1=Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III}} (behind the Colossi of Memnon) * Temple of {{ill|Ramesses IV|en|Ramesses IV|WD=Q1532|lt=Ramesses IV|1=Ramesses IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose II|en|Thutmose II|WD=Q158025|lt=Thutmose II|1=Thutmose II}} * Temple of {{ill|Horemheb|en|Horemheb|WD=Q157995|lt=Horemheb|1=Horemheb}}, {{ill|Ay|en|Ay|WD=Q234187|lt=Ay|1=Ay}}, and {{ill|Tutankhamun|en|Tutankhamun|WD=Q12154|lt=Tutankhamun|1=Tutankhamun}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|en|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|WD=Q656040|lt=Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|1=Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III}} ({{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu|1=Medinet Habu}}) === Abydos === * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses II (Abydos)|de|Tempel des Ramses II. von Abydos|ja|ラムセス2世葬祭殿 (アビドス)|zh|拉美西斯二世葬祭殿 (阿比多斯)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|1=Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}: *Hut‑abdu‑Men‑maat‑Ra* (Ḥwt‑3bḏw‑mn‑mȝʿ.t‑Rʿ) – “House of Men‑maat‑Ra in Abydos” == See also == * {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|en|Thebes, Egypt|WD=Q101583|lt=Thebes, Egypt|1=Thebes, Egypt}} * {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, Egypt}} == Literature == * Martina Ullmann, ''König für die Ewigkeit – Die Häuser der Millionen von Jahren: Eine Untersuchung zu Königskult und Tempeltypologie in Ägypten'', Wiesbaden 2002, ISBN In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple|1=Mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs|1=Pharaoh}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid|1=Pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn|1=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon|1=Colossi of “House of Millions of Years” }} [[File:Karte grabstätten theben west.png|thumb|Temple landscape on the west bank of the Nile at Thebes]] The '''million‑year house''' (also '''house of millions of years, Millionenjahrhaus''') is a term that first appears at the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} and originally denoted a temple for the royal cult. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple|1=Mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Their imagery – both in cult statues and wall reliefs – presents a mystical union of the king with a major deity such as {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|WD=Q58373|lt=Amun|1=Amun}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}, or {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah|1=Ptah}}, believed to guarantee the monarch’s eternal existence.citeturn0 Most million‑year houses once stood parallel to the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}}, opposite the {{ill|Valley of the Kings|en|Valley of the Kings|WD=Q133423|lt=Valley of the Kings|1=Valley of the Kings}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben-West|ja|テーベ西岸|zh|底比斯西岸|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; others were erected at {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis, Egypt|1=Memphis, Egypt}} ({{ill|Amenhotep III|en|Amenhotep III|WD=Q42606|lt=Amenhotep III|1=Amenhotep III}}, {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II|1=Ramesses II}}), {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, Egypt}} ({{ill|Seti I|en|Seti I|WD=Q157918|lt=Seti I|1=Seti I}}, Ramesses II), {{ill|Soleb|en|Soleb|WD=Q1457914|lt=Soleb|1=Soleb}} (Amenhotep III) and {{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Abu Simbel temples|1=Abu Simbel}} (Ramesses II). Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs|1=Pharaoh}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid|1=Pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn|1=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon|1=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, Egypt, Temple of Hatshepsut.jpg|mini|Temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}]] [[Datei:Luxor, Medinet Habu, Egypt, Oct 2004 A.jpg|mini|Entrance to {{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu|1=Medinet Habu}}, temple of {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramesses III|1=Ramesses III}}]] === Thebes‑West === *Rock‑cut temples at {{ill|Deir el-Bahari|en|Deir el-Bahari|WD=Q373909|lt=Deir el-Bahari|1=Deir el-Bahari}}* * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Mentuhotep II|de|Totentempel des Mentuhotep II.|ja|メンチュホテプ2世葬祭殿|zh|蒙图霍特普二世葬祭殿|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|en|Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut|WD=Q660692|lt=Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut|1=Mortuary temple of Hatshepsut}} (*Djeser‑djeseru* – “Holiest of Holies”) * [[:en:Temple of Thutmose III|Mortuary Temple of Thutmose III (Deir el-Bahari)]] *Million‑year houses at {{ill|Qurna|de|el-Qurna|ja|エル・クルナ|zh|古尔纳|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Seti I|en|Mortuary Temple of Seti I|WD=Q375642|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Qurna)|1=Mortuary Temple of Seti I}} * {{ill|Temple of the Ramesside kings (Asasif)|de|Tempel der Ramessiden (Asasif)|ja|ラメシデ朝神殿 (アサシフ)|zh|拉美西德王神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III|1=Thutmose III}} (el‑Qurna) * Temple of {{ill|Siptah|en|Siptah|WD=Q158059|lt=Ramesses Siptah|1=Siptah}} * Temple of {{ill|Amenhotep II|en|Amenhotep II|WD=Q158052|lt=Amenhotep II|1=Amenhotep II}} * The {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}} (temple of Ramesses II) * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose IV|en|Thutmose IV|WD=Q157985|lt=Thutmose IV|1=Thutmose IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Tausret|en|Tausret|WD=Q158012|lt=Twosret|1=Tausret}} * Temple of {{ill|Merneptah|en|Merneptah|WD=Q158043|lt=Merneptah|1=Merneptah}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|en|Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|WD=Q1058753|lt=Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III|1=Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III}} (behind the Colossi of Memnon) * Temple of {{ill|Ramesses IV|en|Ramesses IV|WD=Q1532|lt=Ramesses IV|1=Ramesses IV}} * Temple of {{ill|Thutmose II|en|Thutmose II|WD=Q158025|lt=Thutmose II|1=Thutmose II}} * Temple of {{ill|Horemheb|en|Horemheb|WD=Q157995|lt=Horemheb|1=Horemheb}}, {{ill|Ay|en|Ay|WD=Q234187|lt=Ay|1=Ay}}, and {{ill|Tutankhamun|en|Tutankhamun|WD=Q12154|lt=Tutankhamun|1=Tutankhamun}} * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|en|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|WD=Q656040|lt=Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III|1=Mortuary Temple of Ramesses III}} ({{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu|1=Medinet Habu}}) === Abydos === * {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Ramesses II (Abydos)|de|Tempel des Ramses II. von Abydos|ja|ラムセス2世葬祭殿 (アビドス)|zh|拉美西斯二世葬祭殿 (阿比多斯)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|WD=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|1=Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}: *Hut‑abdu‑Men‑maat‑Ra* (Ḥwt‑3bḏw‑mn‑mȝʿ.t‑Rʿ) – “House of Men‑maat‑Ra in Abydos” == See also == * {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|en|Thebes, Egypt|WD=Q101583|lt=Thebes, Egypt|1=Thebes, Egypt}} * {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, “House of Millions of Years” }} [[File:Karte grabstätten theben west.png|thumb|Temple landscape on the west bank of the Nile at Thebes]] The '''million‑year house''' (also '''house of millions of years, Millionenjahrhaus''') is a term that first appears at the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} and originally denoted a temple for the royal cult. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} it came to refer above all to the royal {{ill|Mortuary temple|en|Mortuary temple|WD=Q927825|lt=mortuary temple|1=Mortuary temple}}, though other shrines of the king’s cult can also be called million‑year houses. Their imagery – both in cult statues and wall reliefs – presents a mystical union of the king with a major deity such as {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|WD=Q58373|lt=Amun|1=Amun}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}, or {{ill|Ptah|en|Ptah|WD=Q146321|lt=Ptah|1=Ptah}}, believed to guarantee the monarch’s eternal existence.citeturn0 Most million‑year houses once stood parallel to the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}}, opposite the {{ill|Valley of the Kings|en|Valley of the Kings|WD=Q133423|lt=Valley of the Kings|1=Valley of the Kings}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben-West|ja|テーベ西岸|zh|底比斯西岸|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; others were erected at {{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|WD=Q5715|lt=Memphis, Egypt|1=Memphis, Egypt}} ({{ill|Amenhotep III|en|Amenhotep III|WD=Q42606|lt=Amenhotep III|1=Amenhotep III}}, {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|WD=Q1523|lt=Ramesses II|1=Ramesses II}}), {{ill|Abydos, Egypt|en|Abydos, Egypt|WD=Q192268|lt=Abydos, Egypt|1=Abydos, Egypt}} ({{ill|Seti I|en|Seti I|WD=Q157918|lt=Seti I|1=Seti I}}, Ramesses II), {{ill|Soleb|en|Soleb|WD=Q1457914|lt=Soleb|1=Soleb}} (Amenhotep III) and {{ill|Abu Simbel|en|Abu Simbel|WD=Q134140|lt=Abu Simbel temples|1=Abu Simbel}} (Ramesses II). Already during their lifetimes the {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|WD=Q37110|lt=pharaohs|1=Pharaoh}} set up a monument where they themselves were venerated. Regular {{ill|barque|de|Barke|ja|聖舟|zh|圣船|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} processions brought the cult image of the relevant god into the shrine. The name “house of millions” alludes to millions of years – i.e. for all eternity – during which the cult should endure.citeturn0 == Historical development == At the start of the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} the *classical* pyramid complex took shape with four components – {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Valley Temple|lt=valley temple|de|Taltempel|ja|谷神殿|zh|峡谷神庙|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Causeway (Ancient Egypt|de|Aufweg|lt=causeway|it|Rampa processionale|cs|Vzestupná cesta}}, mortuary temple, and {{ill|Pyramid|en|Pyramid|WD=Q12516|lt=pyramid|1=Pyramid}} – first realised at the {{ill|Meidum Pyramid|de|Meidum-Pyramide|ja|メイドゥームのピラミッド|zh|梅杜姆金字塔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This layout remained standard into the Middle Kingdom. For security reasons the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} moved royal burials to rock‑cut tombs in the Valley of the Kings and relocated the associated mortuary temples to the edge of the cultivable land. Occasionally the valley temple and causeway can still be traced (e.g. at the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}). The peak of {{ill|El Qurn|en|El Qurn|WD=Q1323811|lt=El Qurn|1=El Qurn}} (“the Horn”), a natural pyramid above the valley, symbolically replaced the Old‑Kingdom pyramid. Million‑year houses of the New Kingdom are attested not only at Thebes but also at sites without royal tombs. == Examples == [[Datei:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|mini|Temple of the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|WD=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}}]] [[Datei:EGYPT-AMENHOTEPIII.JPG|mini|The {{ill|Colossi of Memnon|en|Colossi of Memnon|WD=Q750209|lt=Colossi of Memnon|1=Colossi of Memnon}}]] [[Datei:Thebes, Luxor, Egypt, Temple of Hatshepsut.jpg|mini|Temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}]] [[Datei:Luxor, Medinet Habu, Egypt, Oct 2004 A.jpg|mini|Entrance to {{ill|Medinet Habu|en|Medinet Habu|WD=Q932622|lt=Medinet Habu|1=Medinet Habu}}, temple of {{ill|Ramesses III|en|Ramesses III|WD=Q1528|lt=Ramesses III|1=Ramesses III}}]] ===
